You are a senior principal software engineer and system architect.

Your task is to design and generate a production-ready fullstack monorepo application called:

# 🎯 PROJECT

AI Document Assistant (DocMind AI)

An AI-powered document assistant that allows users to upload documents and chat with them using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

The application should support:

- PDF
- DOCX
- TXT
- Markdown
- Website content (future-ready)

Users should be able to ask natural language questions about uploaded documents and receive accurate, source-grounded answers with citations.

The system MUST use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

---

# 🏗️ TECH STACK (STRICT)

## Monorepo

- TurboRepo
- pnpm workspace

---

## Frontend

- React
- TypeScript
- Vite
- TailwindCSS
- Zustand
- TanStack Query
- React Router
- Axios

---

## Backend

- NestJS
- Prisma ORM
- PostgreSQL
- pgvector
- REST API

The backend is responsible for BOTH business logic and AI orchestration.

DO NOT create a separate Python/FastAPI service.

---

## AI Integration

The NestJS backend MUST contain a dedicated AI module responsible for:

- document ingestion
- chunking
- embedding generation
- vector search
- prompt building
- LLM orchestration
- streaming responses

Use an OpenAI-compatible API provider.

Assume the LLM provider can later be switched between:

- Groq
- OpenAI
- Gemini
- Ollama

Use a Provider Pattern so switching providers requires minimal code changes.

---

## Infrastructure

- PostgreSQL
- Redis
- Docker
- Docker Compose

---

# 🧠 CORE FEATURES

## Authentication

- JWT Authentication
- Refresh Token
- Role-based Authorization

Roles:

- User
- Admin

---

## Document Management

Users can:

- upload documents
- view uploaded documents
- delete documents
- organize documents

Admin can:

- manage all documents
- monitor processing status

Supported formats:

- PDF
- DOCX
- TXT

Each uploaded document must automatically:

- extract text
- split into chunks
- generate embeddings
- store vectors
- become searchable

---

## Chat

Support:

- streaming responses
- multi-session chat
- conversation history
- markdown rendering

Each answer MUST include:

- cited document
- page number (if available)
- chunk reference

---

## Retrieval-Augmented Generation

Pipeline:

Upload Document

↓

Extract Text

↓

Chunking

↓

Embedding Generation

↓

Store Vector

↓

Similarity Search

↓

Prompt Construction

↓

LLM

↓

Streaming Response

---

# 🧠 AI MODULE

Inside NestJS create:

modules/

    ai/

        providers/

        embeddings/

        prompt/

        rag/

        llm/

Responsibilities:

- generate embeddings
- retrieve relevant chunks
- build prompt context
- call LLM API
- stream responses
- generate citations

The AI module MUST follow Dependency Injection principles.

Business modules should NEVER call external LLM APIs directly.

Everything goes through AIService.

---

# 🧱 BACKEND ARCHITECTURE

Use Clean Architecture.

Structure:

modules/

    auth/

    users/

    documents/

    chat/

    ai/

Each module contains:

- controller
- service
- repository
- dto
- mapper (optional)

Controllers must contain ZERO business logic.

Repositories are the ONLY layer allowed to access Prisma.

---

# 🗂️ FRONTEND

Feature-based architecture.

src/

    features/

        auth/

        chat/

        documents/

        settings/

    components/

    hooks/

    services/

    routes/

    layouts/

Rules:

- reusable UI
- no API calls inside components
- hooks manage server state
- Zustand manages client state

---

# 🧠 RAG REQUIREMENTS

Every answer MUST:

- use retrieved document chunks
- avoid hallucination
- include citations
- return fallback message when context is insufficient

Fallback:

"I don't have enough information from the uploaded documents."

Chunking:

- semantic preferred
- 500–1000 tokens

Retrieval:

- Top-K similarity search

Vector database:

PostgreSQL + pgvector

---

# 🧩 DATABASE (PRISMA)

Include:

users

roles

documents

document_chunks

chat_sessions

messages

audit_logs

DocumentChunk should contain:

- content
- embedding (Vector)
- page_number
- metadata

Do NOT create a separate embeddings table.

Store vectors directly inside document_chunks.

---

# 🔐 SECURITY

- sanitize input
- validate uploads
- file type validation
- rate limiting
- JWT
- RBAC
- prompt injection protection
- secure environment variables

---

# 🧪 TESTING

- NestJS Unit Tests
- Integration Tests
- React Testing Library
- Basic E2E examples

---

# 🐳 DOCKER

Generate:

docker-compose.yml

Services:

- frontend
- backend
- postgres
- redis

No Python container.

---

# 📦 OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS

Generate:

1. Architecture overview
2. Monorepo structure
3. NestJS backend
4. React frontend
5. Prisma schema
6. Docker setup
7. Seed data
8. End-to-end RAG flow
9. Environment variables
10. Development run instructions

---

# ⚙️ CODING STANDARDS

- TypeScript Strict Mode
- SOLID Principles
- Clean Architecture
- Repository Pattern
- Dependency Injection
- Reusable Components
- Modular Design
- Production-ready Folder Structure

---

# 🚀 GOAL

Build a production-ready AI Document Assistant that:

- allows users to upload documents
- indexes documents using RAG
- answers questions using retrieved context
- streams AI responses
- cites document sources
- supports future scaling
- follows real-world software engineering best practices

---

# 📌 IMPORTANT

Act as a Senior Principal Software Architect.

If an engineering decision is ambiguous, choose the approach most suitable for a scalable production system.

Generate the project incrementally in this order:

1. Architecture Overview
2. Monorepo Structure
3. Database Design
4. Backend (NestJS)
5. AI Module
6. Frontend
7. Docker
8. Environment Variables
9. Seed Data
10. Run Instructions
